commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a9d8366ea7e92f347ae1c5a9c7bc4d5c0b14d1d0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces Feishu mention-context and log-preview raw truncation with `truncateUtf16Safe` and adds helper tests for the surrogate-boundary case.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +25. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main uses raw UTF-16 `.slice` at the reported Feishu paths, so an astral character at the cut boundary can deterministically leave a dangling surrogate; I did not run tests in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical test fixture cleanup should unblock the diff-caused lint failures without changing the runtime fix.

target.title: fix(feishu): truncate mention name and message preview on UTF-16 boundary
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`formatMentionNameForAgentContext` in `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` truncates
mention display names with `normalized.slice(0, MAX_MENTION_CONTEXT_NAME_LENGTH - 3)`.
When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut point, the raw slice
keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone `\uD83D` in the agent
context ‚Äî malformed UTF-16.

Additionally, the inbound message preview (`ctx.content.replace(/\s+/g, " ").slice(0, 160)`)
and the quoted-content log (`quotedContent?.slice(0, 100)`) have the same
surrogate-splitting risk.

## Why This Change Was Made

The plugin SDK provides `truncateUtf16Safe` for this exact pattern. Other
channel plugins (Discord, Feishu, Telegram, Signal, Mattermost, Slack)
already use it for the same purpose.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Mention context names and debug log previews
now emit valid UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/bot.helpers.test.ts` ‚Äî 18 tests passed

## After-fix Proof

Boundary probe on the fixed code path:

```
formatMentionNameForAgentContext with 76 ASCII + üéâ (U+1F389, 2 units) + "BBB"

normalized.length: 81  (exceeds MAX_MENTION_CONTEXT_NAME_LENGTH = 80)

BEFORE normalized.slice(0, 77):
  chars at 76-77: d83d df89  ‚Üê splits üéâ, produces lone surrogate
  output: "AAA‚Ä¶\uD83D"  ‚Üê broken UTF-16

AFTER truncateUtf16Safe(normalized, 77):
  chars at 76-77: emoji skipped whole (doesn't fit)
  output: "AAA‚Ä¶..."  ‚Üê clean, emoji dropped entirely

‚úÖ All three paths produce well-formed UTF-16.
```

## Tests

`extensions/feishu/src/bot.helpers.test.ts` ‚Äî 3 new tests covering
the straddling-emoji boundary in mention names, short-name pass-through,
and special-characters escaping.
target.author: solodmd
